title: Sri M.H. Vasudeva vs Sri C.Gurunath on 2 February, 2017
body: Sri M.H. Vasudeva vs Sri C.Gurunath on 2 February, 2017 Form No.9 (Civil) Title Sheet for Judgment in Suits z R.P. 91 PRESENT: SRI S.H.HOSAGOUDAR, B.Sc.,LL.B.,[Spl] XXVII Additional City Civil Judge Dated this the 2nd day of February 2017 PLAINTIFF: Sri M.H. Vasudeva Aged 60 years, Son of Late M.S.Hanumanthasa, Residing at No.3, 5th Cross, A.M.Lane, B.V.K.Iyengar Road Cross, Bangalore-560 053. [By Sri HSS, Advocate] /v e r s u s/ DEFENDANTS: 1. Sri C.Gurunath Aged about 60 years, Son of Sri Chinnappa, Residing at No.219/B, 54th Cross, 4th Block, Rajajinagar, Bangalore-560 010. 2. The Assistant Revenue Officer, Bangalore Mahanagara Palike, Ward No.31, Chickpet Divsiion, Purnaiah Chatram Road, Bangalore-560 053. 3. The Bangalore Bruhath Mahanagara Palike, Bangalore Represented by its Commissioner. 2 O.S.121/2010 . D1 - By Sri DSS, Advocate D2, D3 - By Sri HGV, Advocate Date of institution of the : 4/1/2010 suit Nature of the suit : For declaration and possession Date of commencement of : 27/11/2010 recording of the evidence Date on which the : 2/2/2017 Judgment was pronounced. : Year/s Month/s Day/s Total duration 7 - 26 (S.H. Hosagoudar) XXVII ACCJ: B'LORE. Plaintiff has filed this suit against defendants for the relief of declaration declaring that plaintiff is the absolute owner of Schedule 'A' property and put the plaintiff into actual vacant possession of the suit Schedule 'A' property and for mandatory injunction directing the defendants 2 and 3 to cancel /revoke the plan, sanction and license in favour of first plaintiff to put up construction and structure over the suit Schedule 'A' property and also Judgment and Decree of mandatory injunction directing the defendants 1 to 3 O.S.121/2010 . 3 to demolish and to remove the structures put up by the first defendant over Schedule 'A' property and put the plaintiff into vacant possession of the suit Schedule 'A' property and also Permanent Injunction restraining the first defendant from interfering with the plaintiff's peaceful possession and enjoyment of the Schedule 'A' property and cost of the suit. 2. In brief, the plaintiff's case is as under: That the plaintiff is the absolute owner of immovable site property bearing municipal no.14/15- 1 situated at 6th cross road, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar presently 5th cross road formerly known as Kempapura Agrahara, Mariyappanapalya Bangalore with BBMP PID No.31-33-14/15-1 situated within ward no.31 measuring East to West 24 feet and North to South 30 feet which is more particularly described in the Schedule 'A' property. The plaintiff acquired the absolute right, title and possession and interest over the Schedule 'A' property by virtue of registered sale deed dated 3/5/1989 and 4 O.S.121/2010 . he purchased the said property from its previous owner Smt. Bayyamma w/o Late Govindappa. Originally suit Schedule 'A' property along with its larger extent of land belonging to one Varahaswamappa who originally held an extent of land measuring an acre of about 3 acres 20 guntas. The said Varahaswamppa out of the aforesaid land sold an extent of land measuring 90 feet x 90 feet to one Govindappa who is none other than the husband of the plaintiff's vendor by executing registered sale deed dated 1/11/39. Subsequent to the death of said Govindappa his wife Bayyamma has succeeded and inherited aforesaid property which was purchased by her husband late Govindappa. It is submitted that suit Schedule 'A' property comes and situated within the extent of land measuring 90 x 90 feet which was purchased by Govindappa. The original owner Varahaswamappa after selling the aforesaid land to the Govindappa, he sold remaining extent of land to different persons after 5 O.S.121/2010 . forming residential sites in the land and private revenue layout was formed in the land of Varahaswamappa. It is pertinent to note that, subsequently entire area come within the purview of Bangalore City Corporation and Bangalore City Corporation formed new roads and issued katha certificate and other revenue records to the site owners by receiving betterment charges from the site owners. While forming roads by the Bangalore City Corporation, one such road came over the extent of land measuring 90 x 90 feet belongs to Govindappa towards his north to south direction as a consequences of which the land measuring 90 x 90 feet was cut into two pieces of land and in the entire above said land, the road was formed and road so formed by the then Bangalore City Corporation is at present marked and numbered as 5th cross road, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar. The said two piece of land are situated towards eastern side and western side of above said 5th cross road. Subsequent 6 O.S.121/2010 . to the death of Govindappa, his wife Smt.Bayyamma has sold eastern portion of aforesaid land in an extent measuring east to west 37 feet and north to south 77 feet to one Venkataswamy by executing registered sale deed dated 30/5/1977 and after selling the eastern portion, Smt. Bayyamma retained western portion of land which is an extent measuring East to West 24 feet and North to South 90 feet which was later assigned and numbered as municipal number 14/15. The said Smt. Bayyamma paid betterment charges for the western portion of the land to the then Bangalore City Corporation on 4/7/1985. It is submitted that Smt. Bayyamma was the absolute owner of the aforesaid land measuring an extent of East to West 24 feet and North to South 90 feet and she was paying tax in respect of the said land. The said Bayyamma out of an extent measuring East to West 24 feet and North to South 90 feet carved out and formed three sites measuring East to West 24 feet and North to South 30 feet and sold one site to 7 O.S.121/2010 . the plaintiff i.e., Schedule 'A' property and sold remaining two properties to Kashinath and K.N.Sampangiram. Accordingly, three different katha were issued for the said three sites by the then Bangalore Mahanagara Palike on 10/1/94 and plaintiff's site was assigned with municipal no.14/15- 1. After purchase of the suit Schedule 'A' property katha was changed in the name of plaintiff and plaintiff is paying necessary tax and other charges in respect of the suit Schedule 'A' property. Now the suit schedule property comes within purview of Bangalore Mahanagara Palike and situated in Ward No.31. This being the real state of affairs, first defendant herein who claims to be owner of property bearing municipal no.14/2A situated at 8th cross, Mariyappanapalya, Magadi road, Bangalore measuring East to West 32 feet and North to South 40 feet in making vexatious attempts to encroach and squat over entire suit Schedule 'A' property by misrepresentation and misleading the true facts. The property claimed by the 8 O.S.121/2010 . first defendant is morefully described in Schedule 'B' property. The first defendant is claiming right, title and interest over the Schedule 'B' property through his mother Smt. Pushpamma who appears to have purchased Schedule 'B' property under registered sale deed dated 18/3/1964. The property claimed by the plaintiff and first defendant are totally different which are having different boundaries, measurement and locations. The title of the first defendant in respect of Schedule 'B' property is highly suspicious and first defendant is claiming that suit Schedule 'A' property is the suit Schedule 'B' property. The defendant no.1 is wrongly identifying and claiming suit schedule property as his property by creating documents. That the defendant no.1 has forcibly occupied the possession of the suit Schedule 'A' property and is now putting up construction over Schedule 'B' property. It is submitted that subsequent to the death of late Pushpamma, the first defendant herein has 9 O.S.121/2010 . written a letter dated 14/1/1984 to the second defendant and has enquired whether their property is acquired. The second defendant herein on 7/2/1984 has written a letter to the first defendant intimating the fact that the acquisition proceedings in respect of suit Schedule 'B' property is under process. It clearly appears that first defendant's property i.e., Schedule 'B' property is acquired by second and third defendant for some public purpose. It is submitted that to the utter shock and surprise second defendant on 20/2/1989 has issued katha in favour of first defendant in respect of the some property bearing municipal no.14/4 situated at 8th cross, Bhuvaneshwar Nagara, Ward No.32, Bangalore. The first defendant on the basis of the said katha is laying claim over Schedule 'A' property with the help of second and third defendant. There is a dispute with regard to the identity of property and also pertaining to the cross roads of the property. The first defendant is trying to take undue advantage in 10 O.S.121/2010 . these confusion and is falsely laying claim over plaintiff's property. The first defendant in pursuance of his malicious and malafide aims has started putting up construction over Schedule 'A' property with the help of second and third defendant. It is submitted that officials of the City Survey Department of Government of Karnataka have made spot inspections and carried on the measurement of the suit Schedule 'A' property and its adjoining sites and have reported the same to the City Survey Department. The officials of the City Survey Department have measured and demarked the metes and bounds of the Schedule 'A' property and assigned suit Schedule 'A' property with CTS No.3141/3. That Schedule 'A' property which is belonged to plaintiff. Defendant No.1 is no way concerned suit Schedule 'A' property. The first defendant in the process of encroaching land is making hectic attempts to encroach over entire Schedule 'A' property. 11 O.S.121/2010 . It is submitted that plaintiff had filed bare suit for Permanent Injunction against defendant no.1 in O.S.No.5121/95. First defendant herein contested the said suit by filing written statement. After trial said suit came to be dismissed by Judgement and Decree dated 15/11/2006. The plaintiff preferred appeal in RFA No.532/2007 and Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka was pleased to dismiss the same for not complying the office objection. Taking advantage of the situation first defendant has encroached and managed to snatch the possession of Schedule 'A' property. Hence this suit. 3. In response to the summons issued by the Court, defendants appeared through their counsel and defendant no.1 and 3 filed separate written statement. In brief the contents of the written statement filed by defendant no.1 are as under: The suit filed by the plaintiff is not maintainable either in law or on facts. It is false to contend that plaintiff is the owner of Schedule 'A' property. The 12 O.S.121/2010 . present suit is hit by principles of res judicata. The present suit is barred under Order 2 Rule 2 C.P.C . That the plaintiff is not at all in possession of the suit schedule property. It is the plaintiff herein who is laying claim over the Schedule 'B' property under the guise of Schedule 'A' property. That the defendant has been in lawful possession and enjoyment of the Schedule 'B' property. That the plaintiff had filed suit in O.S.No.5121/95 for the relief of Permanent Injunction before City Civil Court against defendant no.1 in respect of Schedule 'B' property and after trial, said suit came to be dismissed. The plaintiff thereafter preferred an appeal in RFA No.522/07 before Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka and same also came to be dismissed. Consequently the Judgment and Decree passed by City Civil Court became final and conclusive. The plaintiff under the present suit is claiming that first defendant is laying claim over Schedule 'A' property under the guise of Schedule 'B' property and infact suit Schedule 'A' property which 13 O. S.121 /2010 . was subject matter of the earlier suit and in view of the dispute between the parties with regard to the actual location of the property in dispute, the said court was pleased to appoint Court Commissioner namely Assistant Executive Engineer of Ward No.31, Bangalore City Corporation, Bangalore for location and reporting as to whether suit Schedule 'A' property is in 6th cross as claimed by the plaintiff or in the 8th cross as claimed by the defendant and said Court Commissioner by making local inspection on 18/2/2005 submitted his report to the Court stating that disputed property in the said suit i.e., Schedule 'A' property is not in 6th cross and it is also reported that 8th cross in Bhuvaneshwari Nagar runs in the South to North direction and joined the main Magadi road which in turn joins the southern side of Magadi road i.e., 8th cross, Mariyappanapalya, Bhuvaneshwari nagar, Bangalore and 5th cross of Magadi road are bifurcated by storm water drainage drawn in the sketch. 14 O.S.121/2010 . It is also stated in the said report that there is no building on the suit schedule property and suit schedule property is not in 6th cross of Mariyappanapalya of Bhuvaneshwari Nagar and disputed property is a vacant site. The City Civil Court on appreciation of the evidence and Commissioner Report recorded his finding that suit Schedule 'A' property which is purchased by the plaintiff under the sale deed dated 22/5/89 is not the suit Schedule 'A' property which is in 8th cross of Magadi Road, Bhuvaneshwar Nagar, Bangalore. It is also recorded that suit Schedule 'A' property is not in the 6th cross of Mariyappanapalya, Bhuvaneshwar Nagar purchased under the said deed and hence defendant no.1 contended that suit is not maintainable. Defendant No.1 further contended that he is the absolute owner in possession and enjoyment of Schedule 'B' property acquired the same in terms of Will dated 6/1/1982 and sale deed dated 16/6/2001. The suit Schedule 'B' property was purchased by 15 O. S.121 /2010 . mother of first defendant under registered sale deed dated 18/3/1964 executed by one Thammaiah and in pursuance of the same all the necessary records in respect of the said property stood in the name of his mother and she was in possession and enjoyment of the same. The first defendant in pursuance of the Will and sale deed acquired Schedule 'B' property and is in possession and enjoyment of the same and all the records stands in his name. The first defendant obtained sanctioned plan and license from the Corporation for construction of building and has taken up construction on the Schedule 'B' property and put up ground floor, first floor and second floor and construction work is nearing completion except plastering of the wall and other minor works inside and outside. The first defendant has invested huge amount to the extent of more than Rs.20 lakhs by availing loan for the said construction. Plaintiff is not the owner of the suit schedule property. The defendant no.1 has not at all encroached Schedule 'A' 16 O. S.121 /2010 . property as contended by the plaintiff. The plaintiff is never in possession of property as claimed by him. On the other hand, defendant no.1 is in possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property and took up construction. The dispute with regard to identity as well as cross roads of the property has already been answered in the earlier suit in favour of this defendant. Hence plaintiff is not entitled for any reiefs as sought for. On these grounds, defendant no.1 prays for dismissal of the suit. 4. In brief the contents of the written statement filed by defendant no.3 are as under: That the suit is not maintainable for the reasons that plaintiff has not issued statutory notice as required under Section 482 (1) of Karnataka Municipal Corporation Act, 1976. The averments made in the plaint that plaintiff is the owner in possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property is denied as false. These defendants submit that they have recorded the name of plaintiff and 17 O.S.121/2010 . defendant no.1 as a kathedars of their respective properties and collecting necessary taxes. The recording of katha in respect of property does not confer title to the party and same is for the purpose of payment of the property tax. The plaintiff is not entitled for any relief. On these grounds defendant no.3 prays for dismissal of the suit. 5. On the basis of the pleadings of the parties, my predecessor in the office has framed following issues and additional issues: (1) Whether the plaintiff proves that he is the absolute owner of the suit schedule A property as per the registered sale deed dated 3/5/1989 as alleged in the plaint? (2) Whether the plaintiff further proves that defendant No.1 has obtained Sanctioned plan and license from defendants 2 and 3 by misrepresenting and has put up the constructions and structures illegally over A schedule property as alleged in the plaint? (3) Whether the plaintiff further proves that the constructions 18 O.S.121/2010 . did by the first defendant over A schedule property is illegal and requires demolition and removal of the structures as alleged in the plaint? (4) Whether the plaintiff further proves the alleged interference into his possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule A property by the defendants? (5) Whether defendant No.1 proves that claim of the plaintiff is barred under the principles of res judicata as contended in the written statement? (6) Whether defendant No.3 proves that the suit of the plaintiff is not maintainable for not issuing statutory notice under Section 482 of KMC Act? (7) Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the relief of declaration as prayed? (8) Whether the plaintiff is entitled for vacant possession of A schedule property as prayed? (9) Whether the plaintiff is entitled for Mandatory Injunction against defendants 2 and 3 as prayed? (10) Whether the plaintiff is further entitled for Mandatory 19 O.S.121/2010 . Injunction against defendants 1 to 3 as prayed? (11) Whether plaintiff is entitled for perpetual injunction against defendant No.1 as prayed? (12) What decree or order? ADDITIONAL ISSUES: (1) Whether plaintiff proves that his suit is well within time? 6. In order to prove his case, plaintiff got examined himself as PW.1 and produced in all 82 documents marked as Ex.P1 to Ex.P82 and closed his side of evidence. On behalf of the defendants, first defendant is examined as DW.1 and got marked 47 documents as Ex.D1 to Ex.D47. 7. Heard arguments on both sides and perused entire records of the case. 8. My findings on the above issues are as under: Issue No. 1) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 2) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 3) ............ In the negative; 20 O.S.121/2010 . Issue No. 4) ............ Does not survive for consideration; Issue No. 5) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 6) ............ In the affirmative; Issue No. 7) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 8) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 9) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 10) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 11) ............ In the negative; Issue No. 12) ............ As per final order; for the following: Addl.Issue No.1)........ In the affirmative; 9. ISSUE NO.1 TO 3 : Now I will consider both these issues together for the sake of brevity and convenience and as evidence is also common. 10. It is the case of the plaintiff that he is the absolute owner of suit schedule 'A' property and he has acquired absolute right, title or possession and interest over the Schedule 'A' property by virtue of registered sale deed dated 3/5/1989 executed by 21 O.S.121/2010 . Smt.Bayyamma w/o late Govindappa. After purchase of the property, katha was changed in the name of plaintiff and he is paying all necessary taxes in respect of Schedule 'A' property and plaintiff's site was assigned with municipal no.14/15-1. 11. Further it is the case of the plaintiff that defendant no.1 is the owner of Schedule 'B' property and title of the first defendant in respect of Schedule 'B' property is highly suspicious and first defendant is wrongly identifying and claiming the suit Schedule 'A' property as his property by creating documents. Further it is the case of the plaintiff that defendant no.1 forcibly acquired portion of Schedule 'A' property and now he is putting up construction over the suit Schedule 'A' property in order to defeat the rights of the plaintiff. 12. Further it is the case of the plaintiff that defendant no.1 has no manner of right, title and interest over the Schedule 'A' property. Further it is 22 O.S.121/2010 . the case of the plaintiff that Schedule 'A' property and Schedule 'B' property are distinct and separate properties and there is a dispute with regard to identity of property and also pertaining to cross road of property and first defendant taking undue advantage is falsely claiming his right over the plaintiff's property. 13. In this case defendant no.1 appeared through his counsel and filed written statement denying the case of plaintiff. Defendant No.1 contended that he is the absolute owner and in possession and enjoyment of Schedule 'B' property and he acquired the same through Will dated 6/1/1982 and sale deed dated 16/6/2001. Defendant no.1 further contended that suit Schedule 'B' property was purchased by his mother under registered sale deed dated 18/3/1964 executed by one Thammaiah and in pursuance of the same, all the necessary records in respect of said property stood in the name 23 O.S.121/2010 . of his mother and she was in possession and enjoyment of the same. Further defendant no.1 contended that in pursuance of the Will and sale deed he acquired Schedule 'B' property and he is in possession and enjoyment of the same and all the records stands in his name. Defendant no.1 further contended that he has obtained sanction plan and license from the Corporation for construction of building and he has taken up construction in the Schedule 'B' property and he has put up ground floor, first floor and second floor and construction was nearing completion. 14. Further defendant no.1 contended that plaintiff is not at all owner and in possession and enjoyment of the property claimed by him at any point of time and plaintiff is wrongly claiming his right over his property. 15. In this case, plaintiff in order to prove his case, he examined himself as PW.1. He filed affidavit 24 O.S.121/2010 . evidence in lieu of his examination-in-chief. In his examination-in-chief, he reiterated the plaint averments. He produced in all 82 documents which are marked as Ex.P1 to Ex.P82. In the cross- examination he admitted that he had filed a suit against defendant no.1 in O.S.No.5121/95 and said suit came to be dismissed after trial. He further admitted that in the said suit Assistant Executive Engineer of Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike was appointed as a Commissioner and he has visited the spot and submitted a report. 16. He further admitted that first defendant claims that his property is situated in 8th cross and he claims that his property situated in 6th cross now it is numbered as 5th cross. He further admitted that he has no claim in respect of the property so situated in 8th cross, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar, Bangalore. 17. In this case, defendant no.1 examined himself as DW.1. He filed affidavit evidence in lieu of 25 O.S.121/2010 . his examination-in-chief. In his examination-in-chief he reiterated the contention taken in the statement. He produced in all 47 documents which are marked as Ex.D1 to Ex.D47. In the cross-examination he denied that plaintiff is the owner of Schedule 'A' property. He further denied that he has illegally encroached property of the plaintiff and put up construction. 18. I have perused entire oral and documentary evidence adduced by both the parties. It appears from the evidence on record that both parties are claiming their right in respect of same schedule property. In this case even though plaintiff contended that Schedule 'A' property and Schedule 'B' property are separate and distinct and he is the owner of Schedule 'A' property but on perusal of the pleadings of the parties and evidence adduced by both the parties it appears that both parties are laying claim in respect of same schedule property. 26 O.S.121/2010 . 19. It is an admitted fact that there was a prior litigation between the same parties in respect of same subject matter and admittedly suit in O.S.No.5121/95 was filed by the present plaintiff against defendant no.1 herein in respect of the same subject matter for the relief of Permanent Injunction. 20. It is also admitted fact that after contest, said suit came to be dismissed. In this case evidence on record shows that thereafter plaintiff herein had preferred RFA No.532/07 before Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka. But, he did not prosecute the appeal and hence said appeal came to be dismissed. 21. In this case, both the parties produced copy of the Judgment passed in O.S.5121/ 95 . It is admitted by plaintiff as well as defendant no.1 that in the said suit matter in dispute between the parties was in respect of identity of property and it has been held by the court that though description of the properties claimed by the respective parties as per 27 O.S.121/2010 . documents appears to be distinct and separate, but at the spot on making local inspection by the court commissioner both parties were laying claim over the site existing at the spot as the site belonging to them and said to have been acquired by them under registered instrument which they have obtained through their vendors. Therefore, the dispute was apparently evident with regard to the identity of property and existence of Schedule 'A' property as described in the said suit by the plaintiff at the spot as described in his document. It is apparently evident from the findings recorded by the said Court in O.S.5121/ 95 that on account of dispute between the parties since it relates to the actual location of the property, a Court Commissioner was appointed i.e., Assistant Executive Engineer attached to the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike Ward No.31 for the purpose of location of the schedule property by making local inspection of schedule property and to submit report to the court and to report at whether 28 O.S.121/2010 . properties so claimed by the plaintiff whether exists in 6th cross as claimed by the plaintiff or in 8th cross as claimed by the defendant. PW.1 in his cross- examination clearly admitted that in the above said suit i.e., in O.S.5121/ 95 , Assistant Executive Engineer attached to BBMP ward No. 31 was appointed as a Court Commissioner and he has visited the spot and given report. Hence, from the evidence on record it is much clear that in the previous suit i.e., in O.S.No.5121/95 Court Commissioner was appointed for making local inspection and to submit report and accordingly Commissioner visited the spot and submitted report. The documents produced by the parties clearly shows that the Court Commissioner after having visited the property in presence of both the parties on 18/2/2005 made a report to the court that disputed property measuring 38.8 feet + 40/2 north to south and 32.6 feet + 32.4/2 feet East to West existing at the spot bounded on the east by Kuppaswamy property; 29 O.S.121/2010 . west by 8th cross of Bhuvaneshwari Nagara, north by property of one Babu and to the south by road is in 8th Cross of Bhuvaneshwari Nagara and not in the 6th cross. Further, Court Commissioner also reported that disputed property is in the 8th cross and not in the 6th cross of Bhuvaneshwari Nagara and said 8th cross runs south to north direction and join 5th cross, Magadi road which in turn joins the southern side of the Magadi road and said 8th cross, Mariyappanapalya Bhuvaneshwari nagara and 5th cross on Magadi road bifurcating the stream water drain. Further Court Commissioner prepared sketch about existing facts of the spot and reported to the court. From the Commissioner Report, it is much clear that Court Commissioner has located the disputed property and Commissioner report clearly reveals that the disputed property belongs to defendant. 22. It is also important to note that, from the findings recorded by the Court in the said earlier 30 O.S.121/2010 . proceedings that objections to the Commissioner Report filed by the plaintiff were overruled and Commissioner Report was accepted as per the orders passed by Court on 6/3/2006. It appears that the plaintiff herein has not challenged the said order. It is found that in earlier proceedings held between the parties, has observed that Court Commissioner while answering instruction no.3 of the plaintiff has said that the property in question shown by the plaintiff do not exist on 6th cross, Mariyappanapalya Bhuvaneshwari nagar, Bangalore and said disputed property is a vacant site. It is also observed by the court that the evidence on record with regard to the identity of property purchased by defendant no.1 tallies with the existing property at the spot with the identification of boundaries as found in the documents and reported by the Court Commissioner. 23. PW.1 in his cross-examination clearly admitted that suit schedule property is situated in 6th 31 O.S.121/2010 . cross, Bhavaneshwari Nagar. 5th cross and 6th cross of Bhuvaneshwari nagar are adjacent with each other. He further admitted that municipal records of Schedule 'A' property and also sale deed show that said property is situated in 6th cross road, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar and he has no documents to show that suit Schedule 'A' property is actually situated in 5th cross presently Bhuvaneshwari nagar. 24. Further, he stated that he do not know where is exactly 8th cross is located. Further he stated that the description of Schedule 'B' property is situated in 8th cross, Mariyappanapalya. But the Court Commissioner Report clearly shows that the property of plaintiff do not exist on 6th cross, Mariyappanapalya Bhuvaneshwari Nagara, Bangalore. PW.1 also in his cross-examination clearly admitted that he has no claim in respect of the property so situated in 8th cross, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar, Bangalore and he do not know that who is in 32 O.S.121/2010 . possession of the property so situated in 8th cross, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar. Thus, from the evidence on record it is much clear that, disputed property is in 8th cross and not in the 6th cross of Bhuvaneshwari Nagar. Admittedly, plaintiff has no claim in respect of the property so situated in 8th cross, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar, Bangalore. Hence plaintiff has failed to prove the existence of his property as pleaded in his plaint and also failed to prove his ownership over the property in question. 25. In this case defendant no.1 produced sale deed. The boundaries furnished in the sale deed of the first defendant dated 18/3/1964 executed in his favour by his vendor Krishnan in respect of property bearing No.14/4 is in 8th cross of Bhuvaneshwari Nagar Magadi Road bounded with east by property belonging to Appayyappa, west by road, north by remaining property belonging to defendant no.1 in the same survey number being the northern portion and 33 O.S.121/2010 . to the south property of Annayyappa which tallies with the boundaries stated by the Court Commissioner in respect of the property existing at the spot which is the disputed property and claimed by the plaintiff also as the property acquired by him under the sale deed. 26. It is also admitted that earlier defendant no.1 herein has filed suit against plaintiff in O.S.No.6707/2007 for the relief of Permanent Injunction restraining the present plaintiff from interfering with his peaceful possession and enjoyment of the property owned and possessed by him. In this case, defendant no.1 has produced certified copies of the Judgment and Decree passed in the said suit which are marked as Ex.D46 and Ex.D47. 27. It is pertinent to note that after trial, the said suit filed by the defendant no.1 herein came to be decreed holding that the defendant no.1 herein is in 34 O.S.121/2010 . possession of the disputed property. Admittedly plaintiff has not challenged said Judgment and Decree passed in the above said suit. As such, the said Judgment and Decree become conclusive and binding on the plaintiff. Hence from the evidence on record it is much clear that the defendant is the owner of disputed property and he is in possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property. 28. In this case plaintiff is laying claim over Schedule 'B' property under the guise of Schedule 'A' property. But claim of the plaintiff is negatived in the previous suit. The Commissioner who was appointed in the earlier suit prepared a sketch in presence of parties and sketch prepared by the Commissioner who is specific and certain with regard to the location of property in question. The Commissioner Report clearly shows that disputed property is belongs to defendants. Hence plaintiff is not entitled to contend that defendant no.1 laying claim over his property. On 35 O.S.121/2010 . the other hand, evidence on record clearly shows that plaintiff who is claiming the Schedule 'B' property under the guise of Schedule 'A' property. But, plaintiff has not adduced any cogent evidence to prove that disputed property is belongs to him. 29. The evidence on record clearly shows that plaintiff is the absolute owner and in possession and enjoyment of the property no.14/4 (14/2A) situated at 8th cross, Mariyappanapalya, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar, Bangalore. Further the documents produced by the defendants clearly shows that suit schedule property was acquired by defendant no.1 through his mother Smt.Pushpamma who acquired the same under registered sale deed dated 18/3/1964 executed by Thammaiah. In pursuance of the same, the possession of the property was delivered in favour of mother of defendant no.1. The entries in respect of property stood in the name of mother of defendant no.1 and she was paying taxes. The documents 36 O.S.121/2010 . produced by defendant no.1 clearly shows that his mother executed a Will on 6/1/1982 bequeathing a portion of property acquired by her measuring East to West 32 feet and North to South 27 feet in his favour. 30. Further documents produced by defendant no.1 also shows that katha of the suit schedule property changed in the name of defendant no.1. Further, evidence on record clearly shows that defendant no.1 purchased remaining portion of property bequeathed in favour of his brother under registered sale deed dated 16/6/2001. Thus, entire disputed property is owned, possessed and is in actual possession. 31. Further evidence on record clearly shows that defendant no.1 has paid tax in respect of the property in question. The documents produced by the defendant no.1 clearly shows that he is the absolute owner and in possession and enjoyment of the property in dispute. It appears from the evidence on 37 O.S.121/2010 . record that plaintiff is laying claim under the guise of Schedule 'A' property over the Schedule 'B' property which is belongs to defendant no.1. 32. In this case even though plaintiff has produced documents but he has not produced exact documents to show that suit Schedule 'A' property belongs to him. Further, plaintiff has failed to prove the existence of property claimed by him. In this case plaintiff also failed to establish that suit Schedule 'A' property and suit Schedule 'B' property are separate and distinct properties and first defendant falsely claiming suit Schedule 'A' property as the suit Schedule 'B' property which is said to be belonging to him. 33. In this case plaintiff claiming that he is the owner of Schedule 'A' property. But documents produced by the plaintiff does not shows that plaintiff is the owner of Schedule 'A' property. On the other hand, documents produced by the defendant no.1 38 O.S.121/2010 . clearly shows that he is the owner of property in question. In the previous proceedings, it is held that defendant no.1 is in lawful possession of the property in question. Hence evidence on record clearly shows that defendant no.1 is in lawful possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property. 34. Further evidence on record clearly shows that plaintiff was not at all in possession of the suit schedule property at any point of time. The findings recorded in O.S.No.5121/95 clearly shows that plaintiff herein was not in possession of the disputed property. Under such circumstances it cannot be said that defendant no.1 has illegally encroached the property of plaintiff as contended by him. 35. In this case, there is a dispute with regard to the identity of property in question. But in the previous suit i.e., in O.S.5121/ 95 it is clearly held that disputed property belongs to defendant no.1. The boundaries mentioned in the sale deed of plaintiff is 39 O.S.121/2010 . tallied with the boundaries mentioned in the Commissioner Report. Hence from evidence on record it is much clear that disputed property is belongs to defendant no.1. Even though both the parties are claiming the properties are to be distinct, but Commissioner Report clearly shows that at the spot on making local inspection, both parties were laying claim over the site existing at the spot as the site belonging to them on the basis of their documents. But, Commissioner Report clearly shows that property in question is belongs to defendant no.1 and there is no such property at the spot as contended by the plaintiff. Hence plaintiff has failed to prove his ownership over the suit schedule property. The evidence on record clearly shows that defendant after obtaining necessary sanctioned plan and license from the Corporation authority has put up a construction and he is in lawful possession and enjoyment of the property in question. Further evidence on record clearly shows that defendant no.1 taken up a 40 O.S.121/2010 . construction over his property which consists of ground, first and second floor in accordance with plan. 36. In this case defendant no.1 has produced sanctioned plan and license which is marked as Ex.D38. It clearly shows that defendant no.1 has legally obtained sanctioned plan and license to put up a construction in his property. PW.1 in his cross- examination admitted that after obtaining plan and license defendant no.1 might have put up construction as shown in Ex.D3. It shows that defendant no.1 after obtaining necessary permission and sanction plan has put up construction in his property. 37. In this case even though plaintiff has contended that after dismissal of appeal before Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka, the defendant no.1 has encroached his property, but he has not produced 41 O.S.121/2010 . any cogent evidence on record to show that defendant no.1 has illegally encroached his property. 38. PW.1 in his cross-examination clearly admitted that he is not in possession of Schedule 'A' property and he do not know that when defendant no.1 took possession of Schedule 'A' property and he has not filed any complaint against defendant with regard to taking possession of the suit schedule property. It is pertinent to note that, if really defendant no.1 has illegally taken the possession of the suit schedule property belongs to plaintiff, then he could have stated on which date defendant no.1 took possession of Schedule 'A' property. But, PW.1 unable to state that when defendant no.1 took possession of Schedule 'A' property. Admittedly, plaintiff has not filed any complaint against defendant no.1 for illegally taking possession of the property in question. The evidence on record clearly shows that at any point of time, plaintiff was in possession of suit schedule 42 O.S.121/2010 . property. The plaintiff is falsely laying his claim over the property of defendants. As already stated in the earlier suit filed by the plaintiff it is clearly held that plaintiff was not in possession of property in question. If plaintiff was really in possession of the Schedule 'A' property as contended by him then plaintiff would not have allowed the defendant no.1 to occupy or put up any construction. Admittedly, plaintiff also not filed any complaint against defendant no.1. This itself is sufficient to show that defendant no.1 has been in possession and enjoyment of the property in question and it is the plaintiff laying claim over the same. Hence evidence on record clearly shows that plaintiff was not at all in possession of the property in question. On the other hand, evidence on record clearly shows that defendant no.1 is in possession and enjoyment of the suit schedule property and he has legally obtained sanctioned plan and license from the Corporation authorities. Under such 43 O.S.121/2010 . circumstances, it cannot be said that defendant no.1 has illegally encroached the disputed property. 39. In this case there is no evidence on record to show that defendant no.1 has obtained sanctioned plan and license by misrepresentation and has put up construction illegally over the property of plaintiff. On the other hand, evidence on record clearly shows that the defendant is the owner of property in question and he has obtained sanctioned plan and license and constructed building in his property. The defendant no.1 has legally put up a construction after obtaining necessary sanctioned plan and license from the competent authority in his property and he has not illegally encroached property of plaintiff as contended by him. Hence question of demolition and removal of structure made in the property in question by defendant no.1 does not arise at all. Plaintiff failed to prove issue no.1 to 3. Accordingly, I answer issue no.1 to 3 in the negative. 44 O.S.121/2010 . 40. ISSUE NO.4: Plaintiff has filed this suit for the relief of declaration and possession. Admittedly, plaintiff is not in possession of the suit schedule property as on the date of filing of this suit. Hence question of alleged interference by the defendant no.1 does not arise at all. This issue has been wrongly framed and hence it does not survive for consideration. Accordingly, I answer issue no.4. 41. ISSUE NO.5: Defendant no.1 contended that earlier plaintiff filed suit in O.S.No.5123/95 and said suit has reached finality and hence present suit is hit by the principles of res judicata. It is an admitted fact that plaintiff herein had filed O.S.No.5123/95 against the present defendant no.1 in respect of same subject matter of the property for the relief of Permanent Injunction. But plaintiff filed this present suit for the relief of declaration and possession. No doubt, in the previous suit and in the present suit, parties and subject matter of the 45 O.S.121/2010 . property are one and the same but relief claimed by the plaintiff in both the suits are different. In the earlier suit, the ownership or title of the parties over the property in question is not at all decided. Hence present suit is not hit by the principles of res judicata. Defendant no.1 failed to prove issue no.5. Accordingly, I answer issue no.5 in the negative. 42. ISSUE NO.6: In this case defendants contended that suit is not maintainable for not issuing statutory notice under Section 482 of Karnataka Municipal Corporation Act, 1976. It appears that prior to filing of this suit, plaintiff has not issued statutory notice to the defendants 2 and 3. However, on perusal of the order sheet, it shows that at the time of filing the suit, plaintiff made an application for dispensing with issuance of statutory notice under Section 482 (1) of the Karnataka Municipal Corporation Act, 1976. This court after hearing has allowed the said IA and issuance of 46 O.S.121/2010 . statutory notice as required under Section 482(1) of Karnataka Municipal Corporation Act, 1976 was dispensed with. Hence suit is not bad for non- issuance of statutory notice as contended by defendant no.3. On the other hand, plaintiff has obtained leave of the Court to dispense with the issuance of statutory notice. Defendant No.3 has failed to prove issue no.6. Accordingly, I answer issue no.6 in the affirmative. 43. ADDITIONAL ISSUE NO.1: Defendant no.1 contended that suit is barred by law of limitation. The plaintiff has filed this suit for the relief of declaration, possession and mandatory injunction and Permanent Injunction. The period of limitation for possession is 12 years. According to plaintiff, cause of action arose on October 2009 when first defendant started putting up construction. As already stated plaintiff has not only sought for relief of declaration and injunction but also sought for the possession of 47 O.S.121/2010 . the suit schedule property. The period of limitation for possession is 12 years. Hence suit is well within time. 44. It is the case of the plaintiff that after dismissal of the RFA No.532/2007 dated 8/10/2007, defendant no.1 has illegally took possession of the property in question and on that day, cause of action arose. Even if we taken said date as a cause of action arose to the plaintiff to file the suit for declaration and possession, then also suit of the plaintiff is well within time since plaintiff has filed the suit in the year 2010 within 12 years from 8/10/2007. Hence suit of the plaintiff is well within time. Accordingly, I answer additional issue no.1 in the affirmative. 45. ISSUE NO.7 TO 11: These issues are pertaining to reliefs sought by the plaintiff and hence same are taken up together for consideration. 46. Plaintiff has filed this suit against defendants for the relief of declaration declaring that plaintiff is the absolute owner of Schedule 'A' property 48 O.S.121/2010 . and put the plaintiff into actual vacant possession of the suit Schedule 'A' property and for mandatory injunction directing the defendants 2 and 3 to cancel /revoke the plan, sanction and license in favour of first plaintiff to put up construction and structure over the suit Schedule 'A' property and also Judgment and Decree of mandatory injunction directing the defendants 1 to 3 to demolish and to remove the structures put up by the first defendant over Schedule 'A' property and put the plaintiff into vacant possession of the suit Schedule 'A' property and also Permanent Injunction restraining the first defendant from interfering with the plaintiff's peaceful possession and enjoyment of the Schedule 'A' property and cost of the suit. 47. While answering issue no.1 it is held that plaintiff has failed to prove his ownership over the suit schedule property. While answering issue no.2, it is held that plaintiff has failed to prove that defendant 49 O.S.121/2010 . no.1 has illegally obtained sanctioned plan and license and put up construction and structure illegally over Schedule 'A' property. Further while answering issue no.3, it is held that plaintiff has failed to prove that first defendant has put up illegal construction in Schedule 'A' property and it requires demolition and removal. From the evidence on record it is much clear that plaintiff is not the owner of Schedule 'A' property. The plaintiff has falsely laying his claim over the property which is belongs to defendant no.1. In the earlier suit, it is already held that plaintiff is not in possession of the property in question. The commissioner report also clearly shows that plaintiff is not the owner of property claimed by him. Further evidence on record clearly shows that the plaintiff has not at all in possession of Schedule 'A' property and at any point of time and his laying claim over Schedule 'B' property which is belongs to defendant no.1 under the guise of Schedule 'A' property. The plaintiff has failed to prove his ownership over the suit schedule 50 O.S.121/2010 . property. Therefore, the plaintiff is not entitled for the relief of declaration in respect of his title over the suit schedule property. Plaintiff also failed to prove that defendant no.1 has illegally or forcibly taken the possession of suit schedule property. On the other hand evidence on record clearly shows that property in question belongs to defendants and the plaintiff has laying claim over the defendant no.1 property. Plaintiff has failed to establish his title over the disputed property. Therefore, plaintiff is also not entitled for the relief of possession as sought for. The commissioner report clearly shows that there is no building and property in question shown by the plaintiff is not in 6th cross, Mariyappana Palya, Bhuvaneshwari Nagar, Bangalore and disputed property is a vacant site. The earlier suit filed by the plaintiff for the relief of injunction in O.S.No.6707/07 also dismissed and appeal filed by the plaintiff also came to be dismissed for not taking further steps. Hence it is much clear that plaintiff was not at all in 51 O.S.121/2010 . possession of the suit schedule property at any point of time. The defendant no.1 has constructed building in his property after obtaining necessary sanctioned plan and license. The defendant no.1 is the owner of property in question. Therefore, plaintiff is not entitled for the relief of mandatory injunction as sought against defendants 1 to 3. The defendant no.1 has legally put up construction in his property. Hence question of passing mandatory injunction directing the defendant no.1 to 3 to demolish and remove the structure put up by the defendant no.1 does not arise at all. Plaintiff failed to prove his title and possession over the suit schedule property. Therefore, plaintiff is not entitled for the reliefs of declaration, possession, mandatory injunction and Permanent Injunction as sought for. In this case plaintiff is not entitled for any relief. Accordingly, I answer issue no.7 to 11 in the negative. 52 O.S.121/2010 . 48. ISSUE NO.12: In view of my above discussions and reasoning on the above issues and additional issues, the suit of the plaintiff is liable to be dismissed. In the result, I proceed to pass the following: The suit of the plaintiff is hereby dismissed. Under the facts and circumstances of the case, there is no order as to costs. Draw decree accordingly. *** [Dictated to the Judgment Writer directly on computer, Script corrected, signed and then pronounced by me, in the Open Court on this the 2nd day of February 2017.] [S.H.
